result.first_principles:
Two logical facts frame everything. (1) Tarski: realizability of a fixed finite UDG is an existential sentence over R, so a finite UDG realizes over R iff it realizes over the real algebraic numbers A; with de Bruijn-Erdos, chi(R^2) = chi(A^2) = sup over real number fields F of chi(F^2). Hadwiger-Nelson IS a number-field staircase question: every jump of chi happens at some finite field degree, and the continuum's only role is to make all fields available at once. (2) The wall is computational, not logical: W3 for a fixed clamp is decidable, but elimination cost is super-exponential (repo: Groebner dead past ~14 vertices).

The detectors say exactly where Euclidean content enters the staircase. Q^2 has chi = 2 by Woodall's valuation coloring: arithmetic kills color at the ground step. R^2 already reaches 5 inside the biquadratic field Q(sqrt3, sqrt11), because compass constructions (circle-circle intersection; two unit circles meet in at most 2 points, a Euclidean rigidity fact false in L^infty) generate exactly quadratic extensions. Hence the entire de Grey/Polymath lineage is compass-constructible: all coordinates live in towers of real square roots. L45/L57 say this 2-tower world is forcing-free: 12 graphs, ~2.29M non-adjacent pairs, all free. Read that as arithmetic data, not graph data: adjacency-locality of forcing is plausibly a property of constructible configurations, not of chi-5 graphs per se.

Theorem R prices the missing clamp at one transversal equation (circumradius = 1) on a flexed realization of H-w. Solving one equation along a real-analytic flex is the Intermediate Value Theorem: the one capability R has that Q, every number field, and the constructible field E all lack (their realization sets are totally disconnected; E is not real closed, so sign changes need not have constructible roots). So the 5-to-6 jump should sit precisely at the constructibility boundary: the tuned parameter is a root of an irreducible eliminant with no mechanism forcing solvability by square roots. Strategy corollary: stop compass-searching; search numerically along flexes and certify a posteriori (interval IVT, PSLQ field recovery, exact verification in the recovered field). Verification stays cheap exactly where elimination is hopeless.

conjectures.name: Constructible Ceiling Conjecture (CCC)
conjectures.statement: Let E be the field of real constructible numbers (the closure of Q under field operations and square roots of positive elements; equivalently the quadratic closure of Q in R). Then: (i) chi(E^2) = 5, where E^2 carries the unit-distance graph (the >= 5 half is a theorem: the de Grey graph lies in Q(sqrt3, sqrt11)^2, a subfield of E^2); (ii) no unit-distance-realizable clamp (a chi = 5 UDG with a non-adjacent forced-different pair, in the L51 sense) has a realization with all coordinates in E; (iii) consequently, if chi(R^2) >= 6 then every finite UDG with chi >= 6 has, in every realization, at least one non-constructible coordinate (a coordinate whose Galois closure over Q is not a 2-group). The 5-to-6 jump of the chromatic staircase happens exactly at the ruler-and-compass boundary.
conjectures.falsifiable_prediction: Three checks, in increasing order of contingency. (a) Runnable today: build random 1-dof unit mechanisms on <= 8 vertices in sympy (constructive circle-intersection towers with one free angle), impose circumradius = 1 on a 3-subset, and factor the univariate eliminant of the tuning parameter; CCC's mechanism predicts the minimal polynomial of the tuned parameter is non-2-group (degree with an odd prime factor, or sympy galois_group for degree <= 6 not a 2-group) in the overwhelming majority of instances. If 2-tower-solvable eliminants are common, the mechanism is wrong. (b) Tower-restricted clamp search: any search for clamp realizations restricted to quadratic towers (exact sympy arithmetic, the way every lineage graph verifies) must fail the Theorem R circumradius equation exactly, for every candidate H-w. A single exact 2-tower clamp realization kills CCC outright. (c) When the certified-numerics pipeline (Conjecture 3) produces a clamp, PSLQ/mpmath.findpoly recovery of its coordinates at 200+ digits yields a minimal polynomial that is not 2-group; if the recovered field is multi-quadratic, CCC dies.
conjectures.why_plausible: Every chi >= 5 graph ever realized is compass-constructible, and L45/L51/L56/L57 show this entire constructible corpus is forcing-free (~2.29M pairs, zero exceptions): constructibility and the absence of the clamp have perfectly co-occurred for the whole history of the problem. Theorem R says the clamp costs one transversality condition on a flex; its solution parameter is a root of a structurally irreducible eliminant, and solvability by square roots would be a Galois coincidence with no generating mechanism. Meanwhile the only field-sensitive upper-bound technology known (Woodall-style valuation colorings) is exactly the kind of arithmetic tool that could plausibly extend a 5-coloring across E but break on non-constructible algebraic points.
conjectures.how_it_could_be_wrong: The de Grey graph itself was an arithmetic miracle at special angles (cos theta = 5/6 type coincidences) inside a tiny biquadratic field; the 5-to-6 jump could be one more designer coincidence inside E, in which case (i) and (ii) both fail. Also (i) has an inaccessible half: no technology exists to 5-color E^2, so chi(E^2) could be 6 or 7 without us being able to tell soon; CCC could be false yet unfalsified for years on its upper-bound side. Finally, if some chi-6 witness has a positive-dimensional realization variety whose real curve carries dense constructible points, (iii) collapses.
conjectures.novelty_claim: I have not seen the chromatic number of the constructible plane proposed as a strict intermediate object between chi(Q(sqrt3,sqrt11)^2) and chi(R^2), nor constructibility/2-group Galois structure proposed as the locus of the 5/6 jump. Nearest prior art: Woodall 1973 (chi(Q^2) = 2), the Polymath16 ring threads (chromatic numbers of Z[omega1, omega3, ...]), and the folklore Tarski transfer chi(R^2) = chi(A^2) for A the real algebraic numbers. All of these grade by ring or by algebraicity; none isolates quadratic-closure as a chromatic threshold.

conjectures.name: Steep Staircase Law (SSL)
conjectures.statement: Define the field-degree staircase d(k) = min [F:Q] over real number fields F with chi(F^2) >= k. Then: (i) chi(Q(sqrt3)^2) = 3 exactly, and chi(F^2) <= 3 for every real field F with [F:Q] <= 3 (so d(3) = 2, witnessed by the unit equilateral triangle, which forces sqrt3 into any hosting field after gauge normalization along a unit edge); (ii) d(4) = 4: no 4-chromatic finite UDG realizes with all coordinates in a quadratic or cubic field; in particular every unit-distance realization of the Moser spindle generates a field of degree exactly 4 over Q; (iii) d(5) = 4 (upper half is de Grey's theorem; the law says no chi-5 UDG fits in degree < 4); (iv) bridging to CCC: if chi(R^2) >= 6 has a finite witness, the minimal hosting field is not contained in the constructible field E. The staircase is steep and short: 2, 3, then 4-and-5 together at degree 4, then 6 only past constructibility.
conjectures.falsifiable_prediction: Primary: the spindle field-degree experiment below decides the spindle instance of (ii) outright (the gauge-fixed realization ideal is 0-dimensional and 7-vertex Groebner is well inside the repo's 14-vertex envelope): if any real solution of the spindle system lies in a quadratic field, SSL dies immediately. Secondary probes: (1) SAT search for 4-chromatic UDGs among unit-distance graphs on ball-bounded point sets of Z[1/2, sqrt3]-type rings inside Q(sqrt3)^2: SSL predicts none exist at any size reachable; one found kills (i). (2) Random rational-point search in sympy on the discriminant-square surface governing unit C5 cycles over Q(sqrt2): finding an odd unit cycle there sets chi(Q(sqrt2)^2) = 3 (consistent with the cap, refines the map); finding a 4-chromatic configuration over any quadratic field kills (ii). (3) Constructive target: a Woodall-style valuation 3-coloring of Q(sqrt3)^2 using the prime above 2 in Z[sqrt3]; if the valuation bookkeeping provably cannot give a proper 3-coloring, the upper half of (i) is in trouble.
conjectures.why_plausible: Woodall's 2-adic mechanism is about conic parametrization of unit vectors over F and should extend to quadratic fields with controlled splitting of small primes; 50 years of constructions have never produced a 4-chromatic UDG without dragging in a degree-4 compositum (sqrt33 = sqrt3 * sqrt11 appears in the spindle, Golomb, and every descendant); cubic fields have no quadratic subfield, hence no unit equilateral triangle after gauge normalization, which caps cheap odd structure. The simultaneous arrival of 4 and 5 at degree 4 (spindle and de Grey in the same biquadratic field) is striking data the law takes at face value.
conjectures.how_it_could_be_wrong: 4-chromatic UDGs can be flexible (spindle plus auxiliary vertices), and a flexible witness's realization curve might have quadratic points even though the rigid spindle does not: (ii) quantifies over all 4-chromatic graphs, and the spindle probe only tests one. The valuation 3-coloring of Q(sqrt3)^2 may fail for concrete arithmetic reasons (bad behavior of the prime 2 in Z[sqrt3]), leaving chi(Q(sqrt3)^2) = 4 and killing (i). Odd unit cycles might exist over cubic fields via the flexible C5 surface, making the [F:Q] <= 3 cap in (i) wrong even while d(4) = 4 survives.
conjectures.novelty_claim: The function d(k) as a named object and the specific law (2, 3, 4-and-5 together, 6 past E) appear nowhere I know. Nearest prior art: Woodall 1973; Johnson's chi(Q^n) results; Polymath16's ring computations (which graded by explicit rings Z[omega_i], not by field degree, and never posed minimality of degree per chromatic class); scattered papers on chi of quadratic extensions of Q^2 if they exist (I cannot rule out a chi(Q(sqrt d)^2) literature item, hence the hedge: the new content is the staircase law and the d(4) = d(5) = 4 coincidence claim, not any single field computation).

conjectures.name: One-IVT Witness Conjecture (W3 certificate normal form)
conjectures.statement: There exists a K4-free 6-critical graph H with at most 50 vertices and a degree-5 vertex w, a 2+3 split (A, B) of N(w), and a real-analytic arc q_theta, theta in [a, b], of unit-distance realizations of H-w (over-braced, hence the flex is singular/symmetry-adapted, not generic) such that the circumradius rho(theta) of the 3-subset B satisfies (rho(a) - 1)(rho(b) - 1) < 0. By Theorem R plus the Intermediate Value Theorem this yields a realizable clamp, and via the repo's machine-checked coupling lift (L24) chi(R^2) >= 6. Moreover the W3 certificate has a normal form of total verification cost under one CPU-second: a square-root-tower presentation of q_theta over Q(theta), interval validation of the arc on [a, b] (all radicands positive, all vertex pairs separated, centers s* != t* separated), and one sign change of one interval-evaluable algebraic function. Auxiliary necessity claim (anti-miracle bet): every realizable clamp has split degree deg_H(w) <= flex(H-w) + 4, and since known over-braced planar unit mechanisms exhibit flex <= 2, realizable clamps have deg_H(w) in {5, 6}; in particular every Mycielski-tower clamp (apex degree >= 23) is non-realizable at every tower height.
conjectures.falsifiable_prediction: (a) Near-term checkable sub-claim: a K4-free 6-critical graph with a degree-5 vertex exists at n <= 40 (SAT-checkable: criticality and chi via cadical on candidates from Mycielski/Zykov hybrids and local moves; failure to find any at n <= 50 starves the conjecture). (b) Pipeline run: numerically embed each candidate H-w (the L52 calibrated embedder exists), filter by SVD rigidity-kernel dimension > 3 at near-solutions, track surviving flexes, and monitor rho(theta): the conjecture predicts at least one candidate shows a certified sign change through 1; it is refuted at the stated size bound if all flexes found have rho bounded away from 1 across the full SAT-generated family at n <= 50. (c) Negative side, runnable now: flex-tracking on the 48-vertex Mycielski clamp's H-w finds flex = 0 at every numeric near-solution, consistent with L52 and with the deg <= 6 necessity claim.
conjectures.why_plausible: Theorem R prices the cheapest clamp at exactly one equation, and one-dof singular mechanisms with a tunable circumradius are the cheapest nontrivial objects in plane rigidity (four-bar linkages, twisted-kagome patches are over-braced yet flex through symmetry). The total emptiness of the lineage (L45/L57) is explained rather than contradicted: compass-built graphs are rigid arithmetic objects with no flex, so a flex-tuned clamp was structurally invisible to every prior search method. The verification/search asymmetry is real: deciding W3 by general Tarski elimination at 48 vertices is astronomically infeasible, while an IVT witness, if one exists, is checkable in milliseconds in mpmath.iv; so the absence of a found clamp to date carries little evidence against existence.
conjectures.how_it_could_be_wrong: Min degree 5 of 6-critical graphs forces H-w over-braced by at least 4 (every vertex of H-w has degree >= 4), and over-braced unit frameworks that BOTH flex AND carry 6-critical chromatic structure may simply not exist at any size, or first exist far beyond 50 vertices; de Grey's omega <= 3 world predicts they never exist. The auxiliary deg <= 6 claim could fail the same way L52's heuristics failed: an arithmetic miracle (a P_510-style coincidence-rich rigid graph) could satisfy 19 cocircularities for free, realizing a high-degree-split clamp with zero flex; the claim is explicitly an anti-miracle bet aligned with CCC, not a derived theorem.
conjectures.novelty_claim: The reduction of the chi >= 6 frontier to a one-sign-change interval certificate (an explicit NP-style witness format for W3, against the EXPSPACE Tarski default), the deg_H(w) <= flex + 4 necessity reading of Theorem R, and the resulting blanket non-realizability prediction for all Mycielski-tower clamps are new sharpenings past this repo's W3_CLAMP_REDUCTION.md (which named the flexible clamp but gave no certificate format and no degree cap). Nearest prior art: Theorem R itself (c:/Users/Owen/dev/hadwiger-nelson/experiments/combinatorial/W3_CLAMP_REDUCTION.md), Hauenstein-Sottile alphaCertified (square systems only; our IVT format sidesteps their over-determined exactness obstruction), and Schulze/Streinu symmetry-adapted rigidity.

experiment.description: Spindle field-degree decision (the first proven riser of the staircase, decisive for SSL(ii)'s spindle instance both ways). Gauge-fix the Moser spindle along a unit edge: p1 = (0,0), p2 = (1,0); this is lossless for field membership because any F-realization can be translated by an F-vector and rotated by the F-entries (cos, sin) of a unit edge vector while staying in F^2. That leaves 10 unknowns and 10 quadric equations (11 edges minus the gauged one). In sympy: (1) compute a lex Groebner basis over QQ and confirm the ideal is 0-dimensional (if positive-dimensional, decompose and analyze components instead; still decisive, slightly more work); (2) extract the univariate eliminant for each coordinate, isolate all real roots (sympy real_roots); (3) for every real solution, compute minimal polynomials of all 10 coordinates (sympy minimal_polynomial) and the degree of the generated compositum; (4) decide: does ANY real unit-distance realization of the spindle have all coordinates in a single quadratic field? Predicted outcome: no; every real solution generates a quartic field (sqrt33-type compositum), giving the first rigorously proven step of the staircase above Woodall: chi >= 4 witnesses of spindle type require field degree 4. Counter-outcome: a quadratic realization exists, killing SSL immediately and redirecting the lens. Serialize all solutions, minimal polynomials, and field degrees as JSON to c:/Users/Owen/dev/hadwiger-nelson/experiments/combinatorial/_cache/spindle_field_degree.json.
experiment.repo_feasibility: Pure sympy, no new dependencies, no C compiler needed. Direct precedent: c:/Users/Owen/dev/hadwiger-nelson/experiments/combinatorial/h2_groebner_moser14.py ran exact Groebner at 14 vertices; this is 7 vertices with 10 unknowns after gauge. Graph data and exact coordinates available via experiments/_shared/unit_distance_graph.py. Real-root isolation and minimal_polynomial are stock sympy. Output JSON fits the repo's cache convention.
experiment.est_cost: Half a day to write (~150 lines); 1 to 4 CPU-hours of sympy runtime; effectively zero risk of infrastructure failure.

detector_self_check.Q2: The engine is the IVT on real-analytic flex arcs plus quadratic-closure bookkeeping. Over Q^2 realization sets are totally disconnected: there are no arcs and no IVT, so the clamp-tuning mechanism is literally undefined there. Better: the staircase framework has chi(Q^2) = 2 BUILT IN as its ground step (Woodall's valuation coloring is the model for all SSL upper bounds), so the proposal predicts chi(Q^2) = 2 rather than risking chi(Q^2) >= 3. Euclidean content enters through completeness of R (sign changes have roots), which is exactly what Q lacks.
detector_self_check.Linf: Every count used here rests on two Euclidean rigidity facts: two unit circles meet in at most 2 points, and cocircularity of k points costs k - 2 equations (the pricing inside Theorem R). In the L^infty plane unit spheres intersect in segments, cocircularity has codimension 0, the rigidity matroid degenerates, and there is no circumradius function: the clamp mechanism and the entire certificate format do not exist there. The staircase conjectures are statements about Euclidean planes over number fields, never about abstract normed planes, so nothing here would push L^infty past its true chi = 4.
detector_self_check.R1: The flex arcs feeding the IVT are rotational: they exist because O(2) acts and circle-circle intersections move continuously under rotation (the free angle in every mechanism tower is a rotation parameter). On R^1 the isometry group has no rotations, unit frameworks have no 1-parameter flexes beyond translation, and no circumradius exists; the tuning object vanishes identically, so no part of the argument constrains chi(R) = 2.
